learning_outcomes.description: Describe the major principles of sustainable development;
Apply the principles of sustainable development to a range of issues, and propose practical improvements;
Critically analyse and debate contentious topics in sustainable development;
Assess the conflicts and trade-offs inherent in sustainable development;
Discuss the interests and roles of multiple stakeholders in promoting sustainable development;
Effectively use verbal and written communication skills to articulate knowledge;
Source, read and demonstrate an understanding of academic literature in relation to sustainable development issues;
Work both in groups and independently toward a better understanding and promotion of sustainable development.

assessments.task_description:
Individual Report (1,200 words)

Students select one SDG and analyze its implementation in a specific country, focusing on trade-offs, policy responses, and local context. Students will apply sustainability concepts to a real-world issue

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Online submission by Turnitin only by the due date and time. No hard copy or assignment cover sheets required.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
Written Project: 9/10/2026 5:00 pm

Group Presentations will take place in the Tutorials in Week 11-12.
assessments.task_description:
Written Project (20%)

2,500-word group report

Presentation (10%)

10-minute presentation plus Q&A

Description:

Student groups develop a sustainability solution for a local, national, or global challenge.

Examples:

  • Sustainable campus initiative
  • Circular economy business model
  • Climate adaptation strategy
  • Sustainable transport plan
  • Waste reduction program

The Project will be submitted online via Turnitin through Blackboard, with one group member submitting on behalf of the group.

The presentation slides must be uploaded to Blackboard before the Presentations in Week 11-12.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description: This assessment is based on students' quality and consistency of engagement throughout the semester. Students will be assessed on their contributions to tutorial discussions, SDG case studies, stakeholder analyses, collaborative activities, sustainability problem-solving exercises, research critiques, policy debates, literature analysis, and collaborative learning activities
assessments.submission_guidelines: There is no student submission. Tutors will record participation progressively using a marking rubric across the semester.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: As this relates to participation across the semester, and there is no student submission, late penalties are not applicable.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
The Final Examination is a comprehensive assessment designed to evaluate students' understanding of the principles and practices of sustainable development covered throughout the course.

The examination will assess students' ability to explain key sustainability concepts, apply sustainability principles to real-world challenges, critically evaluate competing perspectives, assess trade-offs and conflicts, analyse stakeholder interests, and communicate reasoned arguments supported by evidence.

Questions may require students to draw upon examples discussed in lectures, tutorials, readings, case studies, and contemporary sustainability issues at local, national, and global scales.

The examination may include a combination of:

• Multiple-choice questions

• Short-answer questions

• Scenario-based questions

• Applied problem-solving questions

• Short essay questions

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.
